title: skimmia laureola subsp. lancasteri
intro: Skimmia laureola subsp. lancasteri, also known as the Lancashire skimmia, is a rare subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the United Kingdom. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of limonoids and coumarins. The health benefits of Skimmia laureola subsp. lancasteri include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to promote respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and coughs.
